intro: Discord and Plain diverge first on pricing model: Discord uses freemium pricing with a free tier, Plain uses seat pricing with no free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. Discord is built for dev, gaming, and creator communities prioritizing real-time voice and chat engagement and is not a fit for teams wanting searchable, seo-indexed knowledge or formal customer support. Plain is built for apps and is not a fit for products without customer support. On the scoring side, Discord rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; Plain rates 4/5 and 4/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Discord you accept that seo invisible, content locked behind login, while Plain comes with the reality that products without customer support. Neither side is free. Discord's headline strength in the data is that massive free scale with voice, video, chat. Plain's headline strength is that in-app customer support chat. Read each side's best-for and not-for fields carefully before you pick; the shape of your team and workflow matters more than any single score.
quick_take: Discord is for dev, gaming, and creator communities prioritizing real-time voice and chat engagement; Plain is for apps; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Discord if you are dev, gaming, and creator communities prioritizing real-time voice and chat engagement and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose Plain if you are apps and a seat pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.a: Discord uses a freemium model, while Plain uses a seat model; Discord has a free tier and Plain does not. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Discord to Plain?
faq.a: Discord has medium lock-in (data partially exportable via api); Plain has medium lock-in (seat-based, data exportable via api). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Discord; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
In our data, Discord scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Plain. That said, DX is subjective; the Plain score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Plain a good alternative to Discord?
faq.a: Plain is built for apps and explicitly not for products without customer support, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Discord for dev, gaming, and creator communities prioritizing real-time voice and chat engagement, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
